sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.44 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: Let$k(X)$denote the number of conjugacy classes of a finite group$X$. Suppose that a finite group$G=AB$is a product of two subgroups$A,B$of coprime orders. Is it true that$k(AB) k(A)k(B)$? Note that one cannot drop the coprimeness condition and the answer is positive if one of the subgroups is normal, see Archive,~11.43.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
